changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_restrict_egress_ports to k8s_networkpolicy_egress_ports_restricted for consistency
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes NetworkPolicy capabilities, which align with the NIST CM-7-b control by restricting unnecessary network access. The original function names were mostly correct but required minor adjustments for consistency with naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of verb-noun order in function names for clarity
title: Least Functionality - Prohibit or restrict the use...
step1_reasoning: The control CM-7-b focuses on prohibiting or restricting unnecessary network access, which aligns with Kubernetes NetworkPolicies. These policies can enforce least functionality by default denying ingress and egress traffic and restricting the use of specific ports. This ensures that only explicitly allowed traffic is permitted, reducing the attack surface.
